title: [Blastoise] Battle Guide
summary: A balanced 79/100/105 [Water] special [tank] that splits its workload between [rapid-spin] hazard control and clean [ice-beam] cove[rage].
strengths: 79/100/105 bulk lets it take a hit and [return] fire reliably
[rapid-spin] combines hazard removal with a Speed boost
weaknesses: Speed 78 means it usually moves second against attackers
Lacks finishing power until [torrent] triggers below 1/3 HP

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Water] STAB with the highest [sing]le-turn damage
Hazard removal plus a free Speed boost in one slot

recommendedItems.reason: Alternative item to patch the Speed tier

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Blastoise] is a balanced [Water]-type special [tank] that stacks 79/100/105 bulk with clean cove[rage] and [rapid-spin] utility. 85 SpA and 78 Speed look underwhelming, but the [hydro-pump] / [ice-beam] / [aura-sphere] line hits most of the metagame from one slot while doubling as a hazard remover.

## Strengths
HP-invested 100/105 mixed bulk lets [Blastoise] eat a neutral special hit from nearly any top attacker and [return] fire. [modest] plus [leftovers] keeps 85 SpA relevant as chip damage, and the STAB + cove[rage] trio covers [Grass], [Dragon], [Dark], and [Steel] angles that most [Water]s miss. [rapid-spin] clears [stealth-rock] while giving a Speed boost, turning a utility turn into a setup turn.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Grass] and [Electric] hit for 2x and gate [Blastoise] from switching into any competent attacker on those types. Speed 78 is below most unboosted threats, so the Tera call often gets forced early. [torrent] only activates below 1/3 HP, so the damage floor is thin against walls — you must force trades with [ice-beam] or [aura-sphere] before opponents stabilize behind [recover]y.

## Recommended Moveset
[hydro-pump] is the locked STAB because the raw damage keeps [Blastoise] offensively relevant. [ice-beam] is mandatory cove[rage] for [Grass], [Dragon], and [Flying]. [aura-sphere] punishes [Dark] and [Steel] walls that pivot in expecting a free turn. The last slot is [rapid-spin] for hazard control and the Speed boost — [flash-cannon] or [dark-pulse] are viable swaps if the team does not need a spinner.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default item for the long-game plan. [sitrus-berry] is the alternative when one guaranteed heal matters. [choice-scarf] is the offensive variant that turns [Blastoise] into a [revenge] killer. Keep [torrent] for the low-HP damage spike. Tera [Water] preserves STAB, Tera [Fairy] flips [Grass], [Dark], and [Dragon] weaknesses at once.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Blastoise] is the special wall and hazard remover, so pair it with a physical wall on the [Steel] or [Ground] axis. A [Ground]-type partner covers [Electric], and [volt-switch] or [u-turn] pivots bring [Blastoise] in at the right turn. [dragon-dance] and [nasty-plot] sweepers behind it benefit directly from the cleared field.

## Play Patterns
Bring [Blastoise] in on turn two or three — let a pivot eat the early chip, then [rapid-spin] on a predicted switch. Save Tera for a telegraphed [Grass] or [Electric] move, and aim [hydro-pump] in [torrent] range when a game-winning KO is on the line.
